title: Determining Genome Targeting Efficiency using T7 Endonuclease I (M0302)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"14sed","text":"T7 Endonuclease I recognizes and cleaves non-perfectly matched DNA. This protocol describes how to determine genome targeting efficiency by digesting annealed PCR products with T7 Endonuclease I. In the first step PCR products are produced from the genomic DNA of cells whose genomes were targeted using Cas9, TALEN, ZFN etc. In the second step, the PCR products are annealed and digested with T7 Endonuclease I. Fragments are analyzed to determine the efficiency of genome targeting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":17}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"CAA1F6483DE1450EB22ED35645DD0109","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/m0302-t7-endonuclease-i"}}}}

steps: Set up a 50 µL PCR reaction using ~ 100 ng, genomic DNA as a template. For each amplicon set up 3 PCR reactions using the following templates: gDNA from targeted cells (e.g. Cas9, or TALEN transfected cells) gDNA from negative control cells (e.g. non-specific DNA transfected cells) water (i.e. no template control) PCR using Q5 High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase A B C 1 COMPONENT 50 µl REACTION FINAL CONCENTRATION 2 Q5® Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix (M0494) 25 µl 1X 3 10 µM Forward Primer 2.5 µl 0.5 µM 4 10 µM Reverse Primer 2.5 µl 0.5 µM 5 Template DNA variable 100 ng total 6 Nuclease-free water To 50 µl Component 50 µl reaction Final Concentration Q5® Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix (M0494S) 25 µl 1X 10 µM Forward Primer 2.5 µl 0.5 µM 10 µM Reverse Primer 2.5 µl 0.5 µM Template DNA variable 100 ng total Nuclease-free water To 50 µl
Collect all liquid to the bottom of the tube by a quick spin if necessary.

Transfer PCR tubes to a PCR machine and begin thermocycling: Cycling Conditions A B C 1 STEP TEMPERATURE TIME 2 Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 3 35 cycles 98°C 5 seconds 4 *50-72°C 10 seconds 5 72°C 20 seconds 6 Final Extension 72°C 2 minutes 7 Hold 4-10°C *Use of the NEB T m Calculator is highly recommended. Note: Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix does not require a separate activation step. Standard Q5 cycling conditions are recommended.
Analyze a small amount of the of the PCR product to verify size and appropriate amplification.

Purify the PCR reaction using 90 µL, Ampure XP beads following the manufacturer’s recommendations. Other PCR purification systems (e.g. Monarch PCR & DNA Clean Up Kit , or Zymo DNA Clean and Concentrator™) are acceptable.
Elute PCR products in 30 µL, water , recovering 25 µL .
Measure the concentration of the purified PCR products.
Assemble reactions as follows: A B 1 COMPONENT 19 µl ANNEALING REACTION 2 DNA 200 ng 3 10X NEBuffer 2 2 µl 4 Nuclease-free Water To 19 µl

Anneal the PCR products in a thermocycler using the following conditions: Hybridization Conditions A B C D 1 STEP TEMPERATURE RAMP RATE TIME 2 Initial Denaturation 95°C 5 minutes 3 Annealing 95-85°C -2°C/second 4 85-25°C -0.1°C/second 5 Hold 4°C Hold
Add undetermined, T7 Endonuclease I (M0302) to the annealed PCR products for a final volume of 20 µL .
Stop the reaction by adding 1.5 µL, 0.25 M EDTA .

Purify the reaction using 36 µL, Ampure XP beads according to the manufacturer’s suggestion. This step is optional since 1 µl of the reaction will not interfere with analysis on an Agilent Bioanalyzer using DNA1000 reagents.
Elute the DNA fragments in 20 µL, water , recovering 15 µL .
Analyze the fragmented PCR products and determine the percent of nuclease-specific cleavage products (fraction cleaved).
Calculate the estimated gene modification using the following formula: % gene modification = 100 x (1 – (1- fraction cleaved) 1/2 )
